title: Sydney Life Survival Quiz for International Students
description: 50 English Sydney/NSW life-survival multiple-choice questions for international students. correctAnswer is stored as exact option text so answer checking does not depend on option order.
schemaNotes: correctAnswer must exactly match one item in options. Do not use option order or letters for judging.

questions.question: When you arrive in Australia with food, plant material, or animal products, what should you do?
options: Declare them on arrival
Hide them in checked luggage
Only declare them if they are fresh
Give them to a friend before customs
questions.correctAnswer: Declare them on arrival

questions.question: After your Australian residential address changes, who should you normally notify within 7 days as a student visa holder?
options: Your education provider
Your mobile provider
Your supermarket loyalty programme
Your airline
questions.correctAnswer: Your education provider

questions.question: Which option is usually a practical first mobile phone setup after arriving in Sydney with an unlocked phone?
options: A prepaid SIM or SIM-only plan
Buying a new phone is mandatory
Using only campus Wi-Fi for the whole semester
Applying for a driver licence first
questions.correctAnswer: A prepaid SIM or SIM-only plan

questions.question: Which set of items is commonly useful when opening an Australian bank account as a new international student?
options: Passport, visa details, and Australian contact details
A beach towel, Opal card, and grocery receipt
Only a local gym membership
A printed social media profile
questions.correctAnswer: Passport, visa details, and Australian contact details

questions.question: What is OSHC mainly for?
options: Health cover for international students during their stay
A public transport pass
A bank account type
A rental bond payment method
questions.correctAnswer: Health cover for international students during their stay

questions.question: If your passport is lost or stolen in Sydney, what is the best first response?
options: Contact your consulate or embassy and report theft to police if relevant
Ignore it until the end of semester
Borrow a friend's passport
Use your student card as a passport replacement for international travel
questions.correctAnswer: Contact your consulate or embassy and report theft to police if relevant

questions.question: What is the safest approach to a very cheap rental listing before you arrive in Sydney?
options: Verify the listing and avoid paying before proper checks
Transfer the bond immediately
Send passport scans to any advertiser who asks
Trust it if the photos look professional
questions.correctAnswer: Verify the listing and avoid paying before proper checks

questions.question: Which document identifies your enrolled course and is commonly needed for student visa and enrolment processes?
options: Confirmation of Enrolment
Coffee loyalty card
Cinema ticket
Beach parking permit
questions.correctAnswer: Confirmation of Enrolment

questions.question: Which Australian number should you call for an immediate life-threatening emergency?
What is a sensible way to store important arrival documents?
options: Keep secure digital copies and protect the originals
Leave originals loose in shared kitchen drawers
Post all originals overseas immediately
Give originals to a new housemate for convenience
questions.correctAnswer: Keep secure digital copies and protect the originals

questions.question: Which public transport modes are commonly part of Sydney's Opal network?
options: Train, metro, bus, light rail, and ferry
Only taxis
Only long-distance flights
Only private bicycles
questions.correctAnswer: Train, metro, bus, light rail, and ferry

questions.question: When using contactless payment on Sydney public transport, what should you do?
options: Tap on and tap off with the same card or device
Tap on with one card and tap off with another
Only tap on for trains
Only tap off if an inspector asks
questions.correctAnswer: Tap on and tap off with the same card or device

questions.question: What may happen if you forget to tap off on Sydney public transport?
options: You may be charged a default fare
The trip always becomes free
Your visa is automatically cancelled
The station refunds you in cash
questions.correctAnswer: You may be charged a default fare

questions.question: If you are not eligible for a NSW student transport concession, which fare type should you expect to pay?
options: Adult fare
Senior fare
Child fare
Free travel on every service
questions.correctAnswer: Adult fare

questions.question: If you use a Concession Opal card, what should you be ready to show when asked?
options: Valid proof of concession entitlement
A gym pass
A shopping receipt
A friend's student card
questions.correctAnswer: Valid proof of concession entitlement

questions.question: What is the safest behaviour near a train or light rail platform edge?
options: Stand behind the safety line until the service stops
Lean over the edge to check the track
Run along the platform while boarding
Block the doors so friends can catch up
questions.correctAnswer: Stand behind the safety line until the service stops

questions.question: What should you check before relying on public transport late at night?
options: The last service time and any trackwork or service changes
Only the weather forecast
The colour of the station seats
Whether your phone wallpaper matches the route map
questions.correctAnswer: The last service time and any trackwork or service changes

questions.question: What should you do if a bus is replacing trains because of planned trackwork?
options: Follow the replacement bus signs and allow extra travel time
Wait on the train platform without checking updates
Assume all fares are cancelled
Walk on the train tracks
questions.correctAnswer: Follow the replacement bus signs and allow extra travel time

questions.question: Which habit helps avoid accidental double charging with contactless public transport payment?
options: Use one card or device consistently for the whole trip
Switch between wallet cards at random
Tap every card in your wallet
Ask another passenger to tap for you
questions.correctAnswer: Use one card or device consistently for the whole trip

questions.question: What is the best way to plan an unfamiliar Sydney public transport trip?
options: Use an official trip planner or reliable real-time transport app
Guess the route from the train colour
Board the first vehicle that arrives
Ask a random driver to change route
questions.correctAnswer: Use an official trip planner or reliable real-time transport app

questions.question: In NSW, how much rental bond can usually be required for a residential tenancy?
options: Up to 4 weeks' rent
Up to 1 week's rent
Up to 6 months' rent
Any amount the agent requests
questions.correctAnswer: Up to 4 weeks' rent

questions.question: What is the purpose of a condition report when moving into a rental property?
options: To record the property's condition at the start of the tenancy
To replace the lease agreement
To set the public transport fare
To remove the need for paying rent
questions.correctAnswer: To record the property's condition at the start of the tenancy

questions.question: What should you do before returning a condition report?
options: Check it carefully, add issues, take photos, and keep a copy
Sign it without inspection
Throw it away after moving in
Let the previous tenant fill it out
questions.correctAnswer: Check it carefully, add issues, take photos, and keep a copy

questions.question: Which statement best matches NSW rent bidding rules?
options: Agents must not invite offers above the advertised rent
Tenants must always bid above the advertised rent
All rental ads can legally say price by negotiation only
Rent bidding is required for student housing
questions.correctAnswer: Agents must not invite offers above the advertised rent

questions.question: What should you avoid before inspecting or verifying a rental property?
options: Paying bond or rent to an unverified advertiser
Checking the address on a map
Reading the lease carefully
Asking for a written agreement
questions.correctAnswer: Paying bond or rent to an unverified advertiser

questions.question: If a product has a major problem under Australian Consumer Law, what remedy may the consumer be entitled to choose?
options: A refund or replacement
Nothing if the store displays a no-refunds sign
Only a social media apology
A higher price for the same item
questions.correctAnswer: A refund or replacement

questions.question: If you simply change your mind about a product, what is generally true under Australian Consumer Law?
options: There is usually no automatic legal right to a refund
Every store must refund you in cash
The police must arrange the refund
A student card guarantees a refund
questions.correctAnswer: There is usually no automatic legal right to a refund

questions.question: Who can NSW tenants contact for government tenancy information and complaint pathways?
options: NSW Fair Trading
The airline that brought them to Australia
A random social media seller
The weather bureau
questions.correctAnswer: NSW Fair Trading

questions.question: What is a safer way to pay rental bond in NSW?
options: Use the official Rental Bonds Online process where available
Pay cash without a receipt
Transfer money to a personal account with no paperwork
Buy gift cards for the agent
questions.correctAnswer: Use the official Rental Bonds Online process where available

questions.question: What should you do before signing a lease?
options: Read the terms, ask questions, and keep a copy
Sign blank pages to save time
Let the agent keep the only copy
Ignore the rent amount
questions.correctAnswer: Read the terms, ask questions, and keep a copy

questions.question: During study periods, what is the usual work limit for most student visa holders?
options: 48 hours per fortnight
48 hours per week
Unlimited hours every week
No paid work at all
questions.correctAnswer: 48 hours per fortnight

questions.question: During official course breaks, what is generally true about the student visa work hour limit?
options: The 48-hour-per-fortnight cap usually does not apply
Students can only work 1 hour per week
Paid work is always banned
Only weekend work is allowed
questions.correctAnswer: The 48-hour-per-fortnight cap usually does not apply

questions.question: What should you apply for before starting paid work in Australia?
options: Tax File Number
Fishing licence
Library barcode
Surf club membership
questions.correctAnswer: Tax File Number

questions.question: Which agency provides information and help about minimum pay and workplace rights in Australia?
options: Fair Work Ombudsman
Transport for NSW
Australian Border Force only
Local cinema box office
questions.correctAnswer: Fair Work Ombudsman

questions.question: Which statement about international students at work is correct?
options: They generally have the same basic workplace rights as other workers
They can always be paid below minimum wage
They do not need payslips
They can only work for cash
questions.correctAnswer: They generally have the same basic workplace rights as other workers

questions.question: What should you do if an employer says your visa means you can be paid below the minimum rate?
options: Check your rights with the Fair Work Ombudsman
Accept it because students have no rights
Pay the employer a fee to keep the job
Give up your payslips
questions.correctAnswer: Check your rights with the Fair Work Ombudsman

questions.question: What is superannuation in Australia?
options: Retirement savings paid by eligible employers
A public transport ticket
A type of student visa
A rental inspection report
questions.correctAnswer: Retirement savings paid by eligible employers

questions.question: What is the safest way to share your TFN with an employer?
options: Provide it through the employer's official onboarding process
Post it publicly online
Send it to every job advertisement
Write it on a noticeboard
questions.correctAnswer: Provide it through the employer's official onboarding process

questions.question: What should you keep after each paid shift or pay cycle?
options: Payslips or records of hours and pay
Only a verbal promise
A photo of the weather
A bus timetable from last year
questions.correctAnswer: Payslips or records of hours and pay

questions.question: For medical advice that is not an immediate emergency, what is usually better than calling 000?
options: Contact a GP, health service, or nurse advice line
Wait until it becomes life-threatening
Ask a stranger on public transport
Call a restaurant
questions.correctAnswer: Contact a GP, health service, or nurse advice line

questions.question: At patrolled Australian beaches, where should you swim?
options: Between the red and yellow flags
Wherever the waves look biggest
Outside the flags to avoid other swimmers
At night when no lifesavers are present
questions.correctAnswer: Between the red and yellow flags

questions.question: Which number is commonly used for non-urgent police assistance in NSW?
Which number is commonly used for SES storm and flood assistance in NSW?
options: 000 for every minor leak
A supermarket helpline

questions.question: What should you do if someone calls claiming your visa will be cancelled unless you pay with gift cards?
options: Do not pay; verify through official channels and report the scam
Buy the gift cards immediately
Share your banking password
Give them your one-time login codes
questions.correctAnswer: Do not pay; verify through official channels and report the scam

questions.question: Which service is a common Australian channel for reporting scams?
options: Scamwatch
A food delivery app
A beach kiosk
A cinema rewards desk
questions.correctAnswer: Scamwatch

questions.question: What is a safe response to a message asking for your banking password or verification code?
options: Do not share it
Reply with the code quickly
Send a screenshot of your banking app
Post the code in a group chat
questions.correctAnswer: Do not share it

questions.question: If you or a friend is at immediate risk of suicide or serious harm, what should you do?
options: Call 000 immediately
Wait for the next business day
Only post online and hope someone replies
Ignore it to avoid embarrassment
questions.correctAnswer: Call 000 immediately

questions.question: Which number is commonly used for Lifeline crisis support in Australia?
options: 000 for non-urgent counselling only

questions.question: What should you do before clicking a link in a message that claims to be from a bank, delivery company, or government agency?
options: Check the sender and use the official website or app instead
Click immediately because all urgent messages are genuine
Enter your password to see what happens
Forward it to everyone in your class
questions.correctAnswer: Check the sender and use the official website or app instead

questions.question: What is a safer habit when walking home late at night in an unfamiliar area?
options: Use well-lit routes, plan transport, and tell someone you trust
Keep your phone flat and tell nobody
Take shortcuts through isolated areas
Accept lifts from unknown drivers without checking
questions.correctAnswer: Use well-lit routes, plan transport, and tell someone you trust
